title: v0.6.2 Hugging Face CDN/Xet CSP hotfix release notes
summary: v0.6.2 is a patch hotfix over v0.6.1 that preserves the task-first UI release and allows the exact Hugging Face CDN/Xet redirect origin required by committed model downloads in production Content Security Policy.
changes: Adds the verified Hugging Face Xet CDN redirect origin `https://us.aws.cdn.hf.co` to Vercel `connect-src` alongside `https://huggingface.co`.
Keeps direct-route SPA fallback and existing COOP/COEP/Permissions-Policy/security headers intact.
Adds redirect-aware static Vercel tests that require exact model download origins and reject wildcard or broad CSP sources.
Updates model-download CSP troubleshooting and release instructions to verify redirect destinations before patch releases.
unchangedFromV061: No UI, model-pack, profile, adapter, portable-bundle, storage, training, or privacy schema contract changes beyond the deployment CSP header.
No telemetry, analytics, remote logging, account service, or broad third-party network allowance was added.
v0.6.0 and v0.6.1 release artifacts and tags are intentionally left immutable; this patch release supersedes them for production model-download installs.
knownLimitations: Issue #255 remains open for final moderated release-usability participant evidence.
Production accuracy and reference-hardware performance evidence limitations from ADR 0004 and ADR 0005 remain unresolved.
Manual Windows NVDA/Edge and macOS VoiceOver/Safari accessibility matrix rows remain accepted limitations until run outside the Linux agent environment.
releaseAssetsPlanned: GitHub source archives generated by GitHub from tag v0.6.2
speech-web-v0.6.2-dist.tar.gz generated from the tagged commit
verificationPlan: Required CI passes on the v0.6.2 release-prep PR and clean main commit.
Production alias returns direct routes with CSP `connect-src 'self' https://huggingface.co https://us.aws.cdn.hf.co`.
Browser smoke clicks Install model on production, verifies the Hugging Face request, verifies the CDN origin is allowed under production CSP, and observes no CSP console errors.
